paper_title: Urticaceae Pollen Image Classification and Data Augmentation

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing pollen samples from Urticaceae species for imaging and analysis.

steps.description: Pollen grains were collected from all five species of Urticaceae found in the Netherlands.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen grains from Urticaceae species in the Netherlands

steps.description: Fresh material was collected with the help of an experienced botanist during the nettle flowering seasons of 2018 and 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fresh pollen material during flowering seasons

steps.description: To produce palynological reference slides, thecae of open flowers were carefully opened on a microscopic slide using tweezers.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare palynological reference slides from open flower thecae

steps.description: The pollen grains were mounted using a glycerin:water:gelatin (7:6:1) solution with 2% phenol and stained with Safranin (0.002% w/v).
steps.searchable_summary: mount and stain pollen grains for microscopy

steps.description: The system used for imaging was a Zeiss Observer Z1 linked to a Hamamatsu EM-CCD Digital Camera.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of pollen slides using microscopy system

methods.description: Data augmentation techniques applied to increase the variety of pollen images used as input for training a deep learning model.

steps.description: We selected the augmentation options brightness and flip.
steps.searchable_summary: apply brightness and flip augmentations to images

steps.description: Brightness range was set from 0.1 to 2, with < 1 corresponding to a darker image and > 1 to a brighter image.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust image brightness within specified range

steps.description: Horizontal-and vertical flip were also applied randomly.
steps.searchable_summary: apply random horizontal and vertical flips to images

steps.description: In addition, we applied L2 regularization and dropout in our neural network structures to prevent overfitting.
steps.searchable_summary: apply L2 regularization and dropout to neural network
